paper.title_en: Direct Hybridization of Integrated Circuits Cooled to Different Cryogenic Temperatures: Device Design
paper.abstract_en: In this article, we present the design of a hybridization and integration of three integrated circuits, each cooled to different cryogenic temperatures. The integration is facilitated by the use of superconducting, and thermally super-insulating multilayer deposits at the interfaces. These circuits are populated with through-silicon vias and hybridized by bumps, enabling up to 14 000 isolated electrical connections between the lower circuit and the upper circuit. This solution will allow the placement of an electronic readout circuit immediately below a detection or a qubit cryogenic circuit, despite the circuits being cooled to different cryogenic temperatures.
